title: Sithisis
content:
{{Top|rwm|ncl|imp}}
{{Noncanon|legends=1}}
{{FAnom}}

|image=[[File:Sithisis.jpg]]

|published in=''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''<ref name="Sithisis" />

|pages=14<ref name="Sithisis" />
|timeline=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]<ref name="Date">[[Derek Thompson]] stated in the "[[Drawn by the Force]]" department of [[Star Wars Insider 82|''Star Wars Insider'' 82]] that he intended "Sithisis" to portray the "last time" [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] presented himself publicly as Palpatine, which occurs during the events of {{Film|III}}, dated to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>
|series=
|preceded by=*"[[Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops]]" {{C|''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}<ref name="Visionaries">''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''</ref>

{{Quote|"'''Sithisis'''" by Derek Thompson: A disturbing look inside the twisted mind of Darth Sidious.|A description of the comic from [[Star Wars Insider 82|''Star Wars Insider'' 82]]|<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref>}}
"'''Sithisis'''" is a fourteen page wordless [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] comic story created by [[Derek Thompson]] and published in the [[2005]] graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''. The comic portrays the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] conducting a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sith alchemy/Legends|ritual]] in his [[Emperor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center|lair]] that begins with him consuming a [[Sith wyrm|worm]]. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] then perceives several characters, including [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], after which a giant [[Worm/Legends|worm]] emerges from Sidious's [[Synthetic lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]] and devours him whole. The worm then disintegrates and Sidious emerges, having transformed into his persona as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] Palpatine. 

Thompson, a concept artist who worked on [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], wanted to portray a glimpse behind Palpatine's secret life. He had a telephone conversation with [[Ian McDiarmid]], the actor who portrayed the character in the live-action ''[[Star Wars]]'' films, prior to working on the comic. At the time of its release, [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] stated that the story was considered possibly [[canon]], depending on the reader's persective.

==Publisher's summary==
"''This shadowy tale follows Darth Sidious through an arcane and monstrous Sith ritual, where he wallows and writhes in the dark side's embrace, shaping the galaxy around him.''"<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" />''

==Plot summary==
{{Quote|The dark side of the Force is a pathway to many abilities some consider to be unnatural.|Palpatine in ''Revenge of the Sith''|<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref>}}
[[File:Palpatine_evil.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The evil of Darth Sidious]]
In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> The [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], appearing old and wrinkled, wi opens his [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] inside his [[Palpatine's meditation chamber/Legends|meditation chamber]]<ref name="Sithisis" /> in the [[Emperor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center|Chancellor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Complete Locations">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> Sidious [[Telekinesis/Legends|levitates]] towards<ref name="Sithisis" /> a [[Sith Holocron repository/Legends|repository]]<ref name="Complete Locations" /> of [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith Holocrons]] and opens it. Initiating a [[Sith alchemy/Legends|ritual]] of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], he scratches his left hand with a fingernail and lets the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] drip into the repository, from which a yellow [[Sith wyrm|worm]] emerges. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] grabs the [[Worm/Legends|worm]], chews, and swallows it before bending over in pain and then moving towards a large<ref name="Sithisis" /> [[Synthetic lightsaber crystal/Legends|synthetic lightsaber crystal]].<ref name="Complete Locations" /> 

He uses [[Force lightning/Legends|force lightning]] on the [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]], a process overseen by<ref name="Sithisis" /> Sith spirits [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]].<ref name="Ultimate Visual Guide">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]''</ref> The lightning appears to reverberate across the planet and the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. In the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on Coruscant, a group of [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] begin crying, while<ref name="Sithisis" /> [[Senator/Legends|Senator]]<ref name="ROTS" /> [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], observed by the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], bends over in pain. Elsewhere, on a frontier<ref name="Sithisis" /> of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]<ref name="ROTS" /> [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] leads [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] in battle when an explosion knocks him down, causing Sidious to grin.<ref name="Sithisis" />

The [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] then perceives<ref name="Sithisis" /> the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]<ref name="ROTS" /> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] tearing apart an [[LM-432 crab droid/Legends|LM-432 crab droid]] on a battlefield. A range of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] with pained expressions appear in Sidious's crystal, as he levitates in front of it. Then, a gigantic worm bursts out from the crystal, shattering it. Sidious removes his [[Sith apparel|hood]], and raises his [[Limb/Legends|arms]] towards the creature, allowing it to devour him. Lightning emanates from the creature before it begins to disintegrate, and Sidious emerges from its ashes in his<ref name="Sithisis" /> [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]<ref name="ROTS" /> Palpatine persona, appearing younger. He leaves the medical center and meets with a group of [[Red Guard]]s, who are holding his Chancellor robes. Palpatine puts on the robes and walks out smiling, with his right pupil still yellow.<ref name="Sithisis" />

==Development==
{{Quote|I wanted to explore an intimate and unseen aspect of Sith ritual, and I liked the idea of seeing just what Sidious does before he becomes Palpatine for the last time. I always found his character fascinating and felt that a glimpse behind the veil of his secrecy would be cool to see.|Derek Thompson|<ref name="Insider 82" />}}
[[File:Derek_Thompson.png|thumb|left|Derek Thompson, the creator of "Sithisis"]]
"Sithisis" was written,<ref name="Sithisis" /> pencilled, inked, lettered, and colored by [[Derek Thompson]],<ref name="Legends" /> a concept artist who worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. It was created for the graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', which was pitched by author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler|J.W. Rinzler]] as a way for the artists of ''Revenge of the Sith'' to share works that would have otherwise gone unseen.<ref name="Visionaries" /> Each artist was given free reign to explore whichever aspect of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe they wished,<ref name="Comics Companion">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref> including their favorite characters, meaning that Thompson chose to center his comic on Palpatine.<ref name="Blog">{{Blogspot|subdomain=derekmonster|url=2009/09/sithisis-visual-development.html|name=DerekMonster|text=SITHISIS: Visual Development|date=2009-09-03}}</ref>

Having storyboarded for ''Revenge of the Sith'', Thompson had regular interactions with writer and director [[George Lucas]] throughout its production, which he felt gave him additional perspective on its events. Thompson applied his additional understandings into his comic, which he wanted to use to explore a Sith ritual and give a glimpse behind Sidious's public mask of Palpatine.<ref name="Insider 82" /> He enjoyed creating a variety of ideas and sketches while preparing for the comic.<ref name="Blog" /> Prior to Thompson's commencement of work on the comic,<ref name="Insider 82" /> Rinzler arranged for him to have a forty-five minute telephone conversation with [[Ian McDiarmid]], the actor who portrayed Sidious<ref name="Insider 82" /> in the live-action ''Star Wars'' films.<ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]''</ref> The two discussed McDiarmid's impressions and ideas about the character, and the actor complemented some of the drawings Thompson had completed at that point. Thompson also praised McDiarmid's graciousness and insightfulness. The artist worked on the comic from [[2004]] to [[2005]].<ref name="Blog" /> "Sithisis" comprises fourteen pages and is wordless aside from the title, which is additionally given in the [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] script.<ref name="Sithisis" />

==Release and reception==
[[File:Swvis1cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|"Sithisis" was published in ''Star Wars: Visionaries'' (cover pictured).]]
''Visionaries'' was announced on [[StarWars.com]] on [[August 3]], 2004, in a post which also revealed Thompson's involvement.<ref name="Visionaries announcement">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20040803.html|text=Coming Soon: ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref> A later post on [[March 15]], [[2005]] revealed the titles and summaries of each of the eleven stories in ''Visionaries''.<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> The anthology was edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and published as a trade paperback by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005,<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> prior to the premiere of ''Revenge of the Sith'' on on [[May 19]], [[2005]].<ref name="Year by Year" /> "Sithisis" is the fourth story in the anthology, following "[[Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops]]" and preceeding "[[Entrenched]]."<ref name="Visionaries" /> Barlow expressed praise for the variety of stories and artworks in ''Visionaries'', believing that those involved had justified their employment under Lucas.<ref name="Insider 82" /> On [[September 3]], [[2009]], Thompson uploaded a blog post that included a variety of early sketches from the comic and details about its creation.<ref name="Blog" />

The entirety of ''Visionaries'' was later republished by Dark Horse in [[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2|''Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space'' Volume 2]]<ref name="Omnibus" /> on [[October 9]], [[2013]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/18-450/Star-Wars-Omnibus-Wild-Space-Volume-2-TPB|text=Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2 TPB}}</ref> "Sithisis" was later published by [[Marvel Comics]] in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Clone Wars Vol. 4]]''<ref name="Legends" /> on [[August 9]], [[2022]].<ref name="Amazon">{{HachetteBookGroup|url=titles/none/star-wars-legends-epic-collection-the-clone-wars-vol-4/9781302932305|text=Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Clone Wars Vol. 4}}</ref> The story was placed between the [[2014]] arc [[Star Wars: Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir|''Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir'']] and the [[Republic 74|seventy-fourth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' series.<ref name="Legends" />

==Continuity==
{{Quote|…the Sidious story is kinda continuity ("from a certain point of view")…|Leland Chee|<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=125719&start=0675#10095628|thread=Event Timeline and Character Age Discussion|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-03-29|time=11:02 AM}}</ref>}}
[[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] stated on [[March 29]], 2005 on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]] that "Sithisis" would be regarded as possible [[canon]], quoting the line "from a certain point of view"<ref name="SWBoards" /> from the [[1983]] [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']],<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> to iterate his perspective.<ref name="SWBoards" /> Thompson intended the story to portray the last time Sidious presented himself as Palpatine,<ref name="Insider 82" /> indicating that "Sithisis" takes place before or during<ref name="Sithisis" /> the events of ''Revenge of the Sith'', in which Palpatine is [[Showdown on Coruscant|permanently]] disfigured.<ref name="ROTS" />

The reference book ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'', written by [[Ryder Windham]] and published on [[October 5]], 2005,<ref name="DK">{{DK|subdomain=uk|url=nf/Book/BookDisplay/0,,11_1405300620,00.html|text=Star Wars the Ultimate Visual Guide|nolive=1}}</ref> included an image from "Sithisis" of Palpatine overlooked by Sith spirits, with a caption explaining that Palpatine had studied ancient holocrons to learn how to harness dark side energy and use Force lightning. The caption also stated that Palpatine had conducted rituals on Coruscant during the Clone Wars to cause unnerving ripples through the Force. These ripples caused anxiety amongst the Jedi while also increasing Skywalker's hunger for power.<ref name="Ultimate Visual Guide" />

[[File:SWCL_Sith_Medcenter.jpg|thumb|left|300px|A cutaway from ''Star Wars: Complete Locations'' that specified the main setting of "Sithisis"]]
The sourcebook [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']], published on [[October 17]], [[2005]]<ref name="Year by Year" /> portrayed several of the objects that were introduced in "Sithisis," including the meditation chamber, holocron repository, and synthetic crystal, and confirmed that they were located within the Chancellor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center, later named the Emperor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center,<ref name="Complete Locations" /> depicted in the ending of ''Revenge of the Sith''.<ref name="ROTS" /> As a part of the Expanded Universe at the time of publication, "Sithisis"<ref name="Sithisis" /> would later be branded as [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] media in [[2014]].<ref name="Legends announcement">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref>
